title: Beta: You hold three stocks in your portfolio: A, B, and...
summary: Calculate the new portfolio beta after selling a stock and reinvesting in a risk-free or other asset.
prompt: You hold three stocks in your portfolio: A, B, and C. The portfolio beta is 1.50. Stock A comprises 20% of the dollar value of your holdings and has a beta of 1.0. If you sell all of your investment in A and invest the proceeds in the risk-free asset, your new portfolio beta will be?
solution: You hold three stocks in your portfolio: A, B, and C. The portfolio beta is 1.50. Stock A comprises 20% of the dollar value of your holdings and has a beta of 1.0. If you sell all of your investment in A and invest the proceeds in the risk-free asset, your new portfolio beta will be: ### Final Answer: D) 1.300 ### Explanation: 1. **Identify Key Variables**: - Portfolio Beta (\(\beta_p\)): 1.50 - Beta of Stock A (\(\beta_A\)): 1.0 - Proportion of Stock A (\(w_A\)): 20% or 0.20 - Risk-free asset Beta (\(\beta_{rf}\)): 0 (since the risk-free asset has zero beta) 2. **Determine the Contribution of Stock A to the Portfolio Beta**: - Contribution of Stock A to Portfolio Beta: \(w_A \times \beta_A = 0.20 \times 1.0 = 0.20\) - Contribution of Stocks B and C to Portfolio Beta: \(\beta_{BC} = \beta_p - \beta_A \times w_A = 1.50 - 0.20 = 1.30\) 3. **Calculate the New Portfolio Beta**: - Since Stock A is sold and invested in the risk-free asset, the new portfolio beta will only consist of Stocks B and C. - New Portfolio Beta: \(\beta_{new} = \beta_{BC} = 1.30\) ### Conclusion: When you sell your investment in Stock A and invest in the risk-free asset, the new portfolio beta will be 1.300. Therefore, the correct answer is D) 1.300.
